title: Dictionary and case statement in query does not return correct rows for distributed table
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe what's wrong
Using a combination of
- sub select
- distributed table
- dictionary
- case clause
- where clause using the case clause
it's possible to create a scenario where the result set only contains data from the local table even when the distributed table is queried.
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
```
CREATE TABLE default.t0 ON CLUSTER '{cluster}' (
id Int,
c String
)
ENGINE = MergeTree()
ORDER BY id;
CREATE TABLE default.t0_d ON CLUSTER '{cluster}'
AS default.t0
ENGINE = Distributed('{cluster}', 'default', t0, rand());
CREATE DICTIONARY default.d0 ON CLUSTER '{cluster}' (
id Int,
d String
) PRIMARY KEY id
SOURCE (
CLICKHOUSE (QUERY '
SELECT *
FROM (
SELECT toUInt8(1) AS id, ''alpha'' AS d
UNION ALL
SELECT toUInt8(2) AS id, ''beta'' AS d
)
')
)
LAYOUT(FLAT())
LIFETIME(0);
INSERT INTO default.t0_d (id, c) VALUES
(1, 'same'),
(2, 'same');
SELECT
id,
c,
dictGet('default.d0', 'd', id) as d,
CASE
WHEN c = 'same' AND d = 'gamma' THEN 'SHOULD NOT HAPPEN'
WHEN c = 'same' THEN 'SHOULD ALWAYS HAPPEN'
END AS filter_value
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE default_t1 (id INTEGER, c TEXT);
CREATE TABLE default_t1_d AS SELECT * FROM default_t1;
INSERT INTO default_t1_d (id, c) VALUES (1, 'same'), (2, NULL), (3, 'different');
SELECT id, c, CASE WHEN id = 1 THEN 'alpha' WHEN id = 2 THEN 'beta' END AS d, CASE WHEN c IS NULL THEN 'NULL VALUE' WHEN c = 'same' THEN 'MATCH' END AS filter_value FROM default_t1_d;

SELECT id, c, CASE WHEN id = 1 THEN 'alpha' WHEN id = 2 THEN 'beta' END AS d, CASE WHEN c = 'different' AND d = 'alpha' THEN 'SHOULD NOT HAPPEN' WHEN c = 'same' THEN 'SHOULD ALWAYS HAPPEN' END AS filter_value FROM default_t1_d;
key_bug_features: Case clause with distributed table returns incorrect rows
